info.name: News Aggregator API
info.description:
Complete API documentation for News Aggregator - RSS feed processing system with AI-powered content generation.

## Features
- Multi-language support (Vietnamese, English, Spanish)
- Automated RSS ingestion and content extraction
- AI-powered content filtering and article generation
- Comprehensive monitoring and analytics

## Authentication
Most endpoints require JWT Bearer token authentication. Use the `/auth/login` endpoint to obtain a token, then set it in the `authToken` environment variable.

## Environment Variables
- `baseUrl`: API base URL (default: http://localhost:3001/api)
- `authToken`: JWT token obtained from login

item.name: Health Check

request.description: Check if the API server is running and healthy. This endpoint does not require authentication.

item.description: Health check endpoints to verify API availability

item.name: Authentication

exec: console.log('Auth token saved to collection variable');

request.description:
Authenticate user and obtain JWT token. The token is automatically saved to the `authToken` environment variable for subsequent requests.

**Request Body:**
- `email` (string, required): User email address
- `password` (string, required): User password

**Response:**
- `token` (string): JWT token for authentication
- `user` (object): User information

Get information about the currently authenticated user. Requires valid JWT token in Authorization header.

**Response:**
- `id` (number): User ID
- `email` (string): User email
- `name` (string): User name
- Other user profile fields

item.description: Authentication endpoints for user login and profile management

item.name: Sources

request.description:
Retrieve a list of all RSS feed sources configured in the system.

**Response:**
- Array of source objects with id, name, rssUrl, siteUrl, lang, topicTags, trustScore, enabled status, fetch interval, and statistics

Get detailed information about a specific RSS source by its ID.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (number): Source ID

**Response:**
- Full source object with all fields and related statistics

Create a new RSS feed source.

**Request Body:**
- `name` (string, required): Source name (1-255 chars)
- `rssUrl` (string, required): Valid RSS feed URL
- `siteUrl` (string, optional): Website homepage URL
- `lang` (enum, optional): Language - VI, EN, or MIXED (default: MIXED)
- `topicTags` (array, optional): Array of topic tags
- `trustScore` (number, optional): Trust score 0-100 (default: 70)
- `enabled` (boolean, optional): Enable/disable source (default: false)
- `fetchIntervalMinutes` (number, optional): Fetch interval 5-1440 minutes (default: 60)
- `denyKeywords` (array, optional): Keywords to filter out
- `notes` (string, optional): Additional notes (max 1000 chars)

Update an existing RSS source. All fields are optional - only provided fields will be updated.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (number): Source ID

**Request Body:** Same as Create Source, but all fields are optional

Delete an RSS source by ID. This will also remove all associated items.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (number): Source ID

Validate an RSS feed URL before creating a source. Checks if the URL is accessible and contains valid RSS/Atom feed.

**Request Body:**
- `url` (string, required): RSS feed URL to validate

**Response:**
- `valid` (boolean): Whether the feed is valid
- `title` (string): Feed title if valid
- `items` (number): Number of items in the feed
- `error` (string): Error message if invalid

Export all sources as JSON for backup or migration purposes.

**Response:**
- Array of all source objects with complete configuration

item.description: Manage RSS feed sources - create, read, update, delete, validate, and export

item.name: Items

query.description: Filter by item status (NEW, EXTRACTED, FILTERED_OUT, READY_FOR_AI, AI_STAGE_A_DONE, AI_STAGE_B_DONE, USED_IN_POST, REJECTED)

request.description:
Get a list of items with filtering and pagination.

**Query Parameters:**
- `status` (enum, optional): Filter by processing status
- `sourceId` (number, optional): Filter by source
- `limit` (number, optional): Items per page (default: 50)
- `offset` (number, optional): Pagination offset (default: 0)
- `search` (string, optional): Search keyword

**Response:**
- Array of item objects with title, link, publishedAt, status, source info, and AI results

Get detailed information about a specific item by ID, including full content, AI results, and metadata.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (number): Item ID

**Response:**
- Complete item object with source, article content, AI results (Stage A and B), and processing history

Get statistics about items grouped by status.

**Response:**
- Object with counts for each status (NEW, EXTRACTED, READY_FOR_AI, etc.)
- Total items count

query.description: Sort by: importance (default), date, or recent

request.description:
Get ready-to-publish items with complete AI-generated articles (AI_STAGE_B_DONE status only).

This endpoint returns items that have completed Stage B AI processing, including:
- Full article content (300-500 words)
- Summary and bullet points
- Why it matters explanation
- Risk flags and suggested hashtags
- Topic tags and importance scores

**Query Parameters:**
- `sortBy` (enum, optional): importance (default), date, or recent
- `limit` (number, optional): Items per page (default: 20)
- `offset` (number, optional): Pagination offset (default: 0)
- `sourceId` (number, optional): Filter by source
- `topicTag` (string, optional): Filter by topic tag
- `fromDate` (string, optional): Filter from date (ISO format)
- `toDate` (string, optional): Filter to date (ISO format)

**Response:**
- `items` (array): Transformed items with full article and AI metadata
- `total` (number): Total count
- `limit`, `offset`, `sortBy`: Request parameters

item.description: Query and manage content items from RSS feeds with AI processing results

item.name: Admin

request.description:
Manually trigger RSS feed ingestion for all enabled sources. The job runs asynchronously in the background.

**Use Case:** Force immediate check for new content from all RSS feeds

**Response:**
- Confirmation message
- Note about background processing

Manually trigger content extraction for NEW items. Extracts full article content from source URLs.

**Request Body:**
- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum number of items to process (default: 10)

**Use Case:** Process newly ingested items to extract their full content

**Response:**
- Confirmation with limit
- Note about background processing

Manually trigger content filtering for EXTRACTED items. Applies keyword filters and basic content analysis.

**Request Body:**
- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum number of items to process (default: 20)

**Use Case:** Filter extracted content before AI processing

**Response:**
- Confirmation with limit
- Note about background processing

Manually trigger AI Stage A processing for READY_FOR_AI items. Uses GPT-4o-mini to:
- Determine content relevance
- Assign topic tags
- Calculate importance score (0-10)
- Generate one-line summary

**Request Body:**
- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum number of items to process (default: 5)

**Use Case:** AI-powered content filtering and categorization

**Response:**
- Confirmation with limit
- Note about background processing

Manually trigger AI Stage B processing for AI_STAGE_A_DONE items. Uses GPT-4o to generate:
- Full article (300-500 words, natural flowing narrative)
- Comprehensive summary
- Key bullet points
- Why it matters explanation
- Risk flags
- Suggested hashtags

**Request Body:**
- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum number of items to process (default: 3)

**Use Case:** Generate publication-ready articles from filtered content

**Note:** This is the most resource-intensive operation as it uses GPT-4o for long-form content generation.

**Response:**
- Confirmation with limit
- Note about background processing

item.description: Administrative operations to manually trigger processing jobs. All operations run asynchronously in the background.

item.name: Stats

request.description:
Get comprehensive statistics about the content processing pipeline.

**Response:**
- Item counts by status
- Processing rates and throughput
- Success/failure rates
- Average processing times per stage

Get recent activity and processing events in the system.

**Response:**
- Recent items processed
- Processing timeline
- Stage completion events
- Error events

Identify bottlenecks in the processing pipeline.

**Response:**
- Stages with high load
- Processing delays
- Resource constraints
- Recommendations for optimization

item.description: Statistics and analytics about content processing pipeline performance

item.name: Monitoring

request.description:
Get high-level monitoring dashboard overview with key metrics, recent logs, and health status.

**Response:**
- System health summary
- Recent error count
- Active jobs
- Key performance indicators

query.description: Filter by log level (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
Number of logs to return (1-500, default: 100)

request.description:
Query application logs with filtering capabilities.

**Query Parameters:**
- `level` (enum, optional): Log level filter
- `service` (string, optional): Service name filter
- `limit` (number, optional): Items per page (1-500, default: 100)
- `offset` (number, optional): Pagination offset
- `startDate` (date, optional): Start date filter
- `endDate` (date, optional): End date filter

Get statistical summary of logs grouped by level and service.

**Response:**
- Log counts by level (ERROR, WARN, INFO, etc.)
- Log counts by service
- Error rate trends

query.description: Filter by type (COUNTER, GAUGE, HISTOGRAM, SUMMARY)
Number of metrics to return (1-500, default: 100)

request.description:
Query performance metrics with filtering.

**Query Parameters:**
- `name` (string, optional): Metric name filter
- `type` (enum, optional): Metric type filter
- `limit`, `offset`: Pagination
- `startDate`, `endDate`: Date range filter

Get statistical analysis for a specific metric.

**Query Parameters:**
- `name` (string, required): Metric name
- `startDate`, `endDate` (optional): Date range

**Response:**
- Min, max, average, sum values
- Percentiles (p50, p95, p99)
- Trend analysis

Get current system resource metrics.

**Response:**
- CPU usage
- Memory usage
- Disk usage
- Network statistics
- Process information

Get current health status of all services and dependencies.

**Response:**
- Overall system health (healthy/degraded/unhealthy)
- Database connection status
- OpenAI API status
- Job scheduler status
- Individual service health checks

query.description: Number of records to return (1-500, default: 100)

request.description:
Get historical health check records.

**Query Parameters:**
- `service` (string, optional): Filter by service
- `limit`, `offset`: Pagination
- `startDate`, `endDate`: Date range

query.description: Number of traces to return (1-500, default: 100)

request.description:
Query distributed traces for request tracking and performance analysis.

**Query Parameters:**
- `traceId` (string, optional): Specific trace ID
- `name` (string, optional): Operation name filter
- `minDuration` (number, optional): Minimum duration in milliseconds
- `limit`, `offset`: Pagination
- `startDate`, `endDate`: Date range

Get slowest traces to identify performance bottlenecks.

**Response:**
- List of slowest operations
- Duration statistics
- Occurrence frequency

Get statistical summary of all traces.

**Response:**
- Average duration by operation
- Success/failure rates
- Request volume
- Performance trends

Get detailed information about a specific trace including all spans and nested operations.

**Path Parameters:**
- `traceId` (string): Trace ID

**Response:**
- Complete trace with all spans
- Timing breakdown
- Tags and metadata
- Error information if any

item.description: Comprehensive monitoring endpoints for logs, metrics, traces, and system health
